title_en: Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib ash-Shara'i: Organization and Vision
title_ar: بدائع الصنائع في ترتيب الشرائع: التنظيم والرؤية
content_en:
Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib ash-Shara'i (The Masterworks of Craftsmanship in Arranging the Divine Laws) by Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani (d. 587 AH / 1191 CE) announces its organizing principle in its full title: the arrangement (tartib) of the divine laws (shara'i) according to a systematic framework. This emphasis on arrangement distinguishes Al-Kasani's work from the organic organization of earlier Hanafi manuals and gives the work its distinctive pedagogical character.

The 'arrangement' that al-Kasani provides is not merely a reordering of topics but a systematic framework for understanding each legal institution. For every major area of law, he identifies: (1) the conditions required for the legal act to be valid; (2) the pillars (arkan) that constitute the act's essential core; (3) the factors that invalidate the act; and (4) the subsidiary questions (furu') that follow from the basic framework. This explicit analytical structure makes Bada'i as-Sana'i unusually transparent in its organization and unusually useful as an educational text.

Al-Kasani was a student of 'Ala ad-Din as-Samarqandi, the author of Tuhfat al-Fuqaha — a well-regarded Hanafi legal text that itself had a clearer organizational scheme than earlier manuals. Bada'i as-Sana'i is in part a commentary on Tuhfat al-Fuqaha that expands as-Samarqandi's positions with fuller analysis. The teacher-student relationship and the work's commentary structure are embedded in the title's reference to 'arrangement' — al-Kasani is organizing and systematizing the tradition he received from his teacher.

The Hanafi school in Central Asia, where al-Kasani was educated, was characterized by rigorous scholarly organization. The great madrasas of Samarkand and Bukhara produced scholars who thought carefully about how to transmit and organize the school's vast accumulated learning. Al-Kasani's systematic approach reflects this tradition — an approach that values clarity of organization as a scholarly virtue alongside comprehensiveness of coverage.

Bada'i as-Sana'i covers the complete range of Hanafi law: taharah, salah, zakat, sawm, hajj, commercial transactions, family law, criminal law, and judicial procedure. In each area, al-Kasani's systematic framework — conditions, pillars, invalidating factors, subsidiary questions — provides students with a reliable organizational map that makes the material learnable and navigable.

The work's influence extended beyond the Hanafi school: its systematic organizational framework was noted and referenced by scholars of other schools as a model of clear legal organization. In the modern period, its explicit identification of conditions and pillars has made Bada'i as-Sana'i particularly useful for comparative legal analysis — its clear articulation of what the Hanafi school considers essential to each legal institution makes comparison with other schools' frameworks straightforward.
title_id: Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib asy-Syara'i: Susunan dan Visi
content_id:
Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib asy-Syara'i (Karya Agung Kerajinan dalam Menyusun Hukum-Hukum Ilahi) karya Abu Bakr ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani (wafat 587 H / 1191 M) mengumumkan prinsip pengorganisasiannya dalam judul lengkapnya: penyusunan (tartib) hukum-hukum ilahi (syara'i) menurut kerangka yang sistematis. Penekanan pada penyusunan ini membedakan karya al-Kasani dari susunan organik manual-manual Hanafi yang lebih awal dan memberi karya ini karakter pedagogis yang khas.

Penyusunan yang al-Kasani sediakan bukan sekadar pengurutan ulang topik-topik melainkan kerangka sistematis untuk memahami setiap institusi hukum. Untuk setiap bidang hukum utama, beliau mengidentifikasi: (1) syarat-syarat yang diperlukan agar perbuatan hukum sah; (2) rukun-rukun (arkan) yang membentuk inti esensial perbuatan; (3) faktor-faktor yang membatalkan perbuatan; dan (4) pertanyaan-pertanyaan cabang (furu') yang mengikuti dari kerangka dasar. Struktur analitis yang eksplisit ini menjadikan Bada'i as-Sana'i luar biasa transparan dalam susunannya dan luar biasa berguna sebagai teks pendidikan.

Al-Kasani adalah murid dari 'Ala ad-Din as-Samarqandi, pengarang Tuhfat al-Fuqaha — sebuah teks hukum Hanafi yang terkemuka — dan beliau menikahi putri gurunya. Karya Bada'i as-Sana'i sering digambarkan sebagai komentar atas Tuhfat al-Fuqaha, meskipun hal ini terlalu sederhana: ia adalah karya yang berdiri sendiri yang jauh melampaui Tuhfat dalam analisis dan cakupannya, dan yang menetapkan standar barunya sendiri tentang bagaimana hukum Hanafi seharusnya disajikan dan diajarkan.
content_ar: Bada'i as-Sana'i fi Tartib ash-Shara'i (The Masterworks of Craftsmanship in Arranging the Divine Laws) by أبو بكر ibn Mas'ud al-Kasani (d. 587 AH / 1191 CE) announces its organizing principle in its full title: the arrangement (tartib) of the divine laws (shara'i) وفقاً لـ a systematic framework. This emphasis on arrangement distinguishes Al-Kasani's work from the organic organization of earlier الحنفي manuals and gives the work its distinctive pedagogical الأخلاق. The 'arrangement' that al-Kasani provides is not merely a reordering of topics but a systematic framework for الفهم each legal institution. For every major area of law, he identifies: (1) the conditions required for the legal act to be valid; (2) the pillars (arkan) that constitute the act's الجوهري الأساسي; (3) the factors that invalidate the act; and (4) the subsidiary questions (furu') that follow from the الأساسي framework. This explicit analytical structure makes Bada'i as-Sana'i unusually transparent in its organization and unusually useful as an educational text. Al-Kasani was a student of 'Ala ad-Din as-Samarqandi, the author of Tuhfat al-الفقهاء — a well-regarded الحنفي legal text that itself had a clearer organizational scheme than earlier manuals. Bada'i as-Sana'i is in part a commentary on Tuhfat al-الفقهاء that expands as-Samarqandi's positions with fuller analysis. The teacher-student relationship and the work's commentary structure are embedded in the title's reference to 'arrangement' — al-Kasani is organizing and systematizing the التراث he received from his teacher. The الحنفي school in المحوري Asia, where al-Kasani was educated, was characterized by rigorous العلمي organization. The great madrasas of Samarkand and Bukhara produced العلماء who thought carefully about how to transmit and organize the school's vast accumulated learning. Al-Kasani's systematic approach reflects this التراث — an approach that values clarity of organization as a العلمي الفضيلة alongside comprehensiveness of coverage. Bada'i as-Sana'i covers the complete range of الحنفي law: الطهارة, الصلاة, الزكاة, الصيام, الحج, commercial transactions, الأسرة law, criminal law, and judicial procedure. In each area, al-Kasani's systematic framework — conditions, pillars, invalidating factors, subsidiary questions — provides students with a reliable organizational map that makes the material learnable and navigable. The work's influence extended beyond the الحنفي school: its systematic organizational framework was noted and referenced by العلماء of other schools as a model of clear legal organization. In the modern period, its explicit identification of conditions and pillars has made Bada'i as-Sana'i particularly useful for comparative legal analysis — its clear articulation of what the الحنفي school considers الجوهري to each legal institution makes comparison with other schools' frameworks straightforward.
